title: 'Exam doesn't matter, my burqa & identity do': NEET aspirant stopped at Ajmer exam centre
content: A NEET-UG re-examination candidate in Ajmer alleged she was prevented from entering the exam hall unless she removed her burqa and dupatta. Kulsum Bano stated she wore the same attire during a previous attempt and would refuse to take the test if her religious identity was challenged. The re-exam proceeded with stringent security measures nationwide following paper leak allegations.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: The boy monster who wakes up on big occasions
content: Sportspersons are often advised to play according to occasion, to minimise risk when the pressure rises. Sooryavanshi appears to have chosen a different route. Whether it was the World Cup final, the IPL knockout or Sunday's tri-series final, he has trusted the same game that brought him here in the first place. He has trusted the attack and his wrists.

title: India-EU FTA to open nearly entire European market for Indian exports by 2027: Goyal
content: India and the European Union are poised to sign their landmark free trade agreement by December, with implementation expected by early 2027. This pact promises Indian exporters near-zero duty access to the vast European market, opening up significant opportunities. Discussions are also progressing on trade deals with the US and Canada, highlighting India's growing global economic influence.

title: Meta CTO quotes Nvidia CEO: AI won't take your job, but someone using AI might
content: Meta's CTO Andrew Bosworth has admitted to an 'atrocious' rollout of the new Applied AI division, causing chaos and undermining trust. He pledged to improve culture, communication, and career growth, while also warning employees that AI proficiency is crucial for job security. Meanwhile, the company is implementing mouse-tracking software, with no opt-out option for employees.

title: Happy Father’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to make dad feel special
content: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.

title: 'Stand with us': With thalis in hand, CJP continues protest at Jantar Mantar
content: Members of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued their protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over alleged examination irregularities. Despite police directives to vacate, the group, led by founder Abhijeet Dipke, vowed to stay until the minister resigns and justice is served for students. Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk also pledged support, threatening a hunger strike.

title: Satya Nadella's new inner circle: Meet Microsoft's key leaders
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has restructured the senior leadership team that ran Microsoft for decades, replacing it with leaner, startup-style teams built for the AI era. Business Insider reports the SLT is gone, swapped for a five-person corporate group, a 35-strong engineering team and a dedicated Copilot unit. Nadella now reviews AI metrics weekly. Longtime executives including Rajesh Jha, Yusuf Mehdi and Charlie Bell are out as Microsoft's AI reboot reshapes its power structure.

title: Ranveer Singh's Rs 325 crore 'Dhurandhar' fee beats Shah Rukh Khan, Rajinikanth
content: Ranveer Singh has reportedly earned a staggering Rs 325 crore from his latest blockbuster, 'Dhurandhar.' This unprecedented figure, achieved through a profit-sharing deal rather than a flat fee, has placed him ahead of other top Indian stars. The film's massive success across various revenue streams, including digital and satellite rights, underscores the rewarding nature of his bold investment strategy.

title: India beat New Zealand 2-0 to clinch second FIH Women's Nations Cup
content: India defeated hosts New Zealand 2-0 in the final to win their second FIH Women's Nations Cup title and secure promotion to the FIH Women's Pro League. Goals from Navneet Kaur and Sunelita Toppo sealed the victory as India completed an unbeaten campaign. Lalremsiami was named Player of the Match, while Deepika finished as the tournament's joint-highest scorer with six goals.

title: Jilted lover stabs 5 in ex-girlfriend’s Telangana house after rejection
content: A jilted lover's pre-dawn knife attack left five family members injured in Hyderabad after he allegedly broke into his former girlfriend's home. The 22-year-old unemployed man, enraged by the breakup a year ago, reportedly climbed to the second-floor flat and attacked his ex-partner, her parents, sister, and uncle. Neighbours intervened, overpowering the accused who was subsequently arrested and remanded in judicial custody.

title: Anthropic's 3k employees have one question after US 'banned' its most powerful AI models
content: Anthropic's 3,000 employees are still confused nearly a week after the White House gave the company under 90 minutes to pull its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, over vague national security concerns. As The New York Times reported from internal chats, staff suspect they're being targeted rather than reviewed. Triggered by an Amazon paper, the shutdown has left engineers asking whether the Trump administration simply doesn't want Anthropic to exist.

title: '100% sure husband drugged & killed her': Delhi techie’s father accuses son-in-law of ‘murder’
content: A Delhi techie's father has accused his son-in-law of murder following her death at a Mussoorie homestay. The father vehemently denies claims of his daughter being drunk, alleging she was drugged and killed. Police are investigating the suspicious death, with the victim's viscera sent for testing and a postmortem report awaited. The husband claims they consumed alcohol before he found her unconscious and bleeding.

title: NEET aspirant given Abu Dhabi centre takes re-test in Nagpur after NTA correction
content: A NEET-UG 2026 candidate from Nagpur, initially assigned an exam center in Abu Dhabi, successfully appeared for the re-test in his hometown after the NTA revised the location. The issue gained national attention, prompting swift action from the agency. While the NTA stated the city change was made via the candidate's login, the family maintained their preference for local centers. This incident occurred amidst the ongoing NEET-UG 2026 re-examination.

title: 'Amma, Appa, Anna, Sorry': Another NEET aspirant dies before retest in Tamil Nadu
content: A 20-year-old NEET aspirant in Hosur died by suicide on the eve of his retest, reportedly due to immense pressure and fear of failing after three years of preparation. His parents were away when he took the extreme step. A suicide note found expressed his love for his family and his deep-seated anxiety about the exam. Police are investigating the tragic incident.

title: 19-year-old NEET aspirant dies by suicide in Hyderabad
content: A 19-year-old NEET aspirant tragically died by suicide in Hyderabad's Miyapur, police reported Sunday. Found hanging in her apartment, she left a note stating no one was responsible. Investigators suspect intense pressure from the NEET re-examination, scheduled for Sunday, may have led to this devastating outcome. The incident highlights the immense stress faced by students preparing for competitive exams.

title: Rap, rhyme, satire: CJP’s second show of dissent in Delhi
content: Artists and young people gathered in Delhi for the Cockroach Janta Party's protest, using rap, poetry, and memes to criticize the NEET-UG paper leak and governance issues.  Demanding the Union Education Minister's resignation, the group chanted slogans and performed songs. Despite tighter security and police requests to disperse, protesters continued their demonstration, vowing to persist until their demands are met.

title: Why India, among largest economies, doesn’t rank in top 50 world’s most powerful passports
content: Despite India's robust economic growth, its passport ranks surprisingly low globally, hovering around the 80th position. This reflects a slower expansion of visa-free access compared to other nations. Factors like diplomatic ties, reciprocal agreements, and perceived security influence rankings, not just economic might. India's progress is gradual, requiring strategic diplomatic efforts and strengthened internal systems to boost international trust and improve its travel freedom.

title: Started at Rs 5,000 salary, now worth Rs 70 lakh: Bengaluru techie's regret
content: A Bengaluru tech professional's 12-year career journey, starting with a ₹5,000 monthly salary and no degree, has culminated in a ₹45 lakh annual package and an estimated ₹70 lakh net worth. Despite setbacks like a 2025 layoff, the individual's honest account of gradual growth, career breaks, and self-doubt resonates widely, highlighting the path to a high-paying tech role.

title: Does your landlord want your Aadhaar, PAN or salary slips? Documents that you are required to share
content: Renting a house? Landlords often ask for extensive financial documents, but the Model Tenancy Act of 2021 clarifies tenant obligations. Only basic identification like Aadhaar and PAN cards are legally required for the Rent Authority. Financial history such as salary slips and bank statements are not mandatory under the Act, though landlords may use them for private vetting. Remember, states must adopt this framework for it to be legally binding.

title: 'Best of luck for NEET re-test, join us after exam': CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continued its protest at Jantar Mantar for a second day, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over NEET exam irregularities. Founder Abhijeet Dipke urged supporters to join after NEET re-tests, while alleging authorities cut water supply to restrooms. The CJP claims their campaign is gaining traction, emphasizing their peaceful pursuit of justice for affected students amidst ongoing political scrutiny of the examination.

title: Sooryavanshi scores fastest List A fifty, but 324.14 strike rate isn't his best
content: Young prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, just 15, delivered a sensational 94 off 29 balls in the Tri-Nation Series final against Sri Lanka A. This explosive innings, featuring a world-record 11-ball fifty, highlights his immense talent.  However, it's not his fastest knock, with a 144 off 42 balls against UAE in 2025 holding that record, showcasing his consistent brilliance.

title: No women on Artemis III? Nasa faces criticism over all-male Moon mission crew
content: NASA faces criticism for selecting an all-male crew for the upcoming Artemis III mission, despite the program's stated commitment to diversity. While the agency defends the choice based on mission requirements and astronaut expertise, critics argue it undermines the goal of landing the first woman on the Moon. The mission itself is a crucial test phase for future lunar landings, not a landing mission.

title: Maa Inti Bangaaram box office: Samantha's film crosses Rs 14.42 cr
content: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaaram' is holding its ground at the box office, raking in an estimated Rs 1.57 crore on its third day. The film has garnered a total of Rs 14.42 crore net in India so far.  Despite a moderate Sunday, the movie's strong opening weekend performance and consistent footfall indicate a steady theatrical run. Critics have praised Samantha's compelling portrayal and the film's emotional depth.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, hits fastest fifty in List A cricket
content: Indian prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a historic feat in the Tri-Nation Series final, smashing the fastest fifty in List A cricket history. The 15-year-old opener blazed to his half-century in a mere 11 deliveries against Sri Lanka A. This electrifying performance, marked by powerful hitting, shattered the previous world record and showcased the young talent's remarkable prowess on the international stage.

title: UK detains Indian captain of sanctioned ship, family in Uttarakhand pleads for release
content: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.

title: Toxic release date: Yash confirms worldwide launch in August
content: Yash's much-awaited action film 'Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups' has a confirmed worldwide release date of August 26, 2026. The star himself announced the news via Instagram with a striking new poster. This date follows several previous schedule adjustments for the Geethu Mohandas directorial, which boasts a star-studded cast including Nayanthara and Kiara Advani. Expectations remain high for this grand cinematic project.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Is your landlord not returning security deposit? Know your rights and what counts as ‘damage’
content: Resolving security deposit disputes in India is all about knowing your rights as a tenant and the duties of landlords. The law makes a clear distinction between normal wear and tear—covered by landlords—and tenant-caused damages. To support claims for deductions, landlords must provide strong evidence, including photos and receipts, to facilitate a fair and efficient resolution between parties involved.

title: 'Development, security, prosperity linked to sea': PM Modi commissions 3 naval ships
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commissioned three indigenously built naval ships – INS Dunagiri, INS Sanshodhak, and INS Agray – in Kolkata, marking a significant stride in India's maritime self-reliance. These vessels, boasting over 75% indigenous content, will bolster the nation's naval prowess in combat, surveying, and anti-submarine warfare, reinforcing India's growing influence on the global maritime stage.

title: NEET paper leak claim: Caller shows ‘question paper’ on video call, demands Rs 30k
content: Ajmer police are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the NEET question paper. A student reported receiving a video call from an unidentified person displaying the paper and demanding Rs 30,000. A local ABVP member also received a similar offer. Authorities are working to identify the caller and confirm the paper's authenticity, suspecting a fraudulent scheme ahead of the NEET re-exam.

title: CJP overnight protest: Dipke claims authorities cut off water, electricity at Jantar Mantar
content: Protesters at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination controversies, face water supply issues in restrooms. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) highlighted the lack of basic facilities for demonstrators, contrasting it with the minister's situation. Meanwhile, the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination is underway nationwide, with CJP inviting aspirants to join their protest post-exam.

title: Who will take wickets in middle overs? Gill pins hope on tall fast bowlers
content: India's search for consistent middle-over wickets has prompted a shift in strategy, with Shubman Gill backing tall fast bowlers like Gurnoor Brar, Prasidh Krishna and Harshit Rana to shoulder the responsibility. The captain explained why India is prioritising pace, bounce and flexibility over fixed bowling roles ahead of the 2027 World Cup.

title: 1.4L CCTVs, 51k jammers, 22.8L candidates: NEET re-test under massive security cover
content: Over 22 lakh students are appearing for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-exam today across India and abroad. The National Testing Agency has deployed extensive security measures, including CCTVs and jammers, at 5,440 centres. Authorities are investigating an alleged attempt to sell the question paper in Ajmer. Meanwhile, a student's exam centre location dispute in Abu Dhabi is also being addressed.

title: How Suvendu Sarkar, raised without parental love, became mother and father to his kids
content: Suvendu Sarkar, who lost his mother at two and was abandoned by his father, found solace in his own family until tragedy struck again in 2021 when he lost his wife to COVID-19. Left to raise his infant son and daughter alone, Suvendu defied doubts and societal expectations, embracing the roles of both mother and father.

title: Room makes record for most saves as Curacao earn first-ever FIFA WC point
content: The result also confirmed Germany's place in the knockout stage after their 2-1 win over Ivory Coast earlier in the day.

title: How to increase the resale value of your house: Tips for homeowners
content: Homeowners can significantly boost their property's selling price with smart, cost-effective upgrades. Focus on enhancing curb appeal with a clean exterior and well-maintained garden. Inside, a fresh coat of neutral paint and deep cleaning create a brighter, more spacious feel. Prioritizing kitchen and bathroom updates, along with essential repairs, builds buyer confidence. Energy-efficient features also add appeal, but avoid over-improving beyond neighbourhood expectations to ensure a better return.

title: Bhiwandi food poisoning: 79 people still hospitalised; FDA cracks down on eateries
content: Patient samples have been sent for microbiological, chemical, and culture analysis to determine the exact cause of the illness

title: Yoga is India’s gift to world, guiding humanity amid challenges: President Murmu
content: Droupad Murmu led International Yoga Day celebrations, performing exercises along with Madhya Pradesh Governor Mangubhai Patel, Chief Minister Mohan Yadav and thousands of participants at a state-level event in Jabalpur

title: Panel sets deadline for Meghalaya govt. to stop ‘faceless’ illegal coal trade
content: The High Court of Meghalaya appointed the Justice B.P. Katakey committee in April 2022 to monitor and regulate the State’s coal sector

title: Meghalaya police told to hunt down financiers of illegal coal trade
content: The crackdown in East Jaintia Hills was ordered ahead of the High Court of Meghalaya’s summons to the administrative and police chiefs of the district on February 9

title: Hyderabad marks International Day of Yoga with mass sessions across city
content: Describing yoga as a symbol of India’s eternal wisdom, Telangana Governor Shukla said it had evolved into a global movement promoting health, harmony and human well-being

title: Cockroach Janta Party protest updates | Protest continues for second day; Abhijeet Dipke urges people to join
content: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters asserted that they would not leave until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns

title: EU FTA puts India on a level playing field against competitors in labour-intensive sectors: chief negotiator
content: Chief negotiator Darpan Jain also said that, apart from an overall benefit to all States, the FTA provides sectoral benefits to several States such as Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Kerala, Gujarat, Maharashtra, among others

title: Congress makes strong pitch for making right to vote a fundamental right
content: Congress leader said that the ECI’s “blatantly partisan functioning” under PM Modi and Amit Shah having been exposed, calling for the elevation of the right to vote to a fundamental right for greater judicial protection

title: Union Minister Kishan Reddy urges Tamil Nadu to support critical mineral projects
content: Union Minister Kishan Reddy says that the revenue generated through the mining of these minerals would go to the State govt, including the premium, royalty, DMF and the cess

title: India-EU trade pact to be signed by December; implemented from Feb-Mar next year: Piyush Goyal
content: Goyal also said that his U.S. Trade Representative, Jamieson Greer, is coming to India this week to hold trade pact talks with him

title: Yoga is more than a physical exercise, it’s a source of consciousness, vitality, and inner energy: PM
content: The Prime Minister observed that when we speak of yoga for healthy ageing, it signifies a collective commitment to ensuring that advancing age does not diminish human potential.

title: Madras High Court takes up suo motu case to protect people from stray dogs in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry
content: The case has been taken up in compliance of a directive of the Supreme Court which had held that compassion for animal life cannot be interpreted in a manner that compels citizens to endure recurring threats to their own lives

title: Two children among four hospitalised after stray dog bite in Chennai
content: Police said the residents in Nagathamman Koil Street of Sanjay Gandhi Colony have been complaining about stray dog menace in the locality in recent times

title: In just four months of 2026, Tamil Nadu records 2.63 lakh dog bites, 17 deaths
content: A total of 62,000 dog bite cases were recorded each in January and February, and it rose to 71,000 in March and dipping slightly to 68,000 in April; half of last year’s deaths has been recorded in just four months this year

title: Yoga has become world’s largest community celebration, says PM Modi
content: PM Modi said that this year’s theme for International Day of Yoga is ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’ and said that the ancient practice plays a crucial role in promoting physical health, mental well-being and active ageing

title: Author-columnist Chandregowda’s ‘Kalamavana Kadedinagalu’ launched in Shivamogga
content: The occasion makes speakers recall characters of ‘Bayaluseeme Kattepurana’, a column which appeared in Lankesh Patrike for over two decades

title: Mysuru records over 1.21 lakh trees in citizen-led census
content: A public data portal is being planned to enable residents and others access the tree database, search for trees by ward or species and report concerns relating to tree health. The database will eventually be handed over to the Mysuru City Corporation as a living record of the city’s green infrastructure.

title: Biscuits instead of breakfast for students: BJP criticises TVK-led government
content: Rejecting that the shortage in food was due to the increase in the number of students, BJP state unit president said the government should have planned depending on the number of students

title: NEET-UG 2026: 2.2 million aspiring medical students undergo re-exam amid tight security
content: The examination is being held from 2.00 p.m. to 5.15 p.m., with the provision of an additional 15 minutes this time

title: News in Frames | Bringing life back to a thirsty land
content: Once known for water scarcity, Rayadurgam in Andhra Pradesh is witnessing an ecological revival through community-led water conservation, afforestation efforts

title: Enumeration finds over 2,000 open wells in the vicinity of reserve forests in Manapparai and Thuvarankurichi
content: The detailed exercise was carried out in the wake of instances of wild animals, such as the Indian Gaur and the spotted deer, having fallen into open wells

title: Two members killed, 58 injured at wedding party as bus plunges into irrigation tank near Rajapalayam
content: The deceased were identified as A. Selvam (55) and K. Kamatchi (50) of Kunnoor; the injured were rushed to the Government hospital in Rajapalayam

title: PM Modi visits President Murmu’s in-laws’ village, explores tribal traditions and way of life
content: President and PM inaugurate and lay foundation stones for development projects worth more than ₹47,000 crore, including a ₹25,016 crore coal gasification unit, ₹6000-crore power projects and ₹700-crore railway infrastructure works

title: Strong maritime capabilities deciding factor for country’s economic, strategic influence: PM Modi
content: West Bengal Governor R.N. Ravi, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, and Indian Navy Chief Admiral Krishna Swaminathan were among the dignitaries present at the commissioning event

title: India emerging as 'trusted partner' to the world: PM Modi
content: Mr. Modi also said that this is a defining period in India's journey, where aspirations are reaching new heights

title: CJP protest in Delhi: Dipke, supporters continue sit-in overnight at Jantar Mantar; urge people to join protest
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who joined the protest on June 20, announced that he would go on a hunger strike on June 27 if Union Education Miniser Dharmendra Pradhan did not step down

title: Permanent yoga centres to be set up in A.P.’s Swarna Grama and Ward Secretariats: CM Naidu
content: He was joined by Baba Ramdev at Yoga Day event in Vijayawada; the CM said Prime Minister Modi has brought global recognition to yoga

title: Inclement weather does not dampen spirits of yoga enthusiasts in Vizianagaram
content: Collector S. Ram Sundar Reddy, Joint Collector Sedhu Madhavan, and other officials performed yoga asanas at Balaji junction, the main venue for the Yoga Day celebrations in the district

title: Thousands join International Yoga Day events worldwide
content: International Day of Yoga is celebrated globally on June 21 every year, since its adoption by the United Nations in 2014

title: Indian man jailed for over 5 years in U.K.-France people smuggling case
content: Jaskirat Singh was prosecuted by the U.K.'s Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) over his role in facilitating the illegal movement of non-British nationals between December 2024 and March 2026

title: ED attaches over ₹1,000-crore assets in Goa 'illegal' iron ore mining case
content: The agency alleged that its probe found AVS Group operated ten mining leases during 2007-12 and generated proceeds of crime of ₹2,492.95 crore from the illegal extraction, sale and export of iron ore

title: Cyber police book controversial YouTuber Thoppi for allegedly posting obscene content
content: Thoppi allegedly posted nude images of his friends on social media after he had a fallout with the latter

title: Thousands perform yoga at Mysore palace as city celebrates International Yoga Day
content: The grand yoga session, held under this year’s theme, ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’, showcased Mysuru’s lasting legacy as a global hub of modern yoga and drew participation from people across all age groups

title: Punjab Police busts gangster module linked to foreign-based handlers; three held
content: According to the investigation, the accused were transporting consignments of weapons and carrying out criminal activities at the behest of their handlers abroad

title: Regular yoga practice makes healthy ageing possible: AIIMS
content: With the world’s elderly population rapidly expanding, making healthy ageing a public health priority is more critical than ever, says the AIIMS Professor

title: Day 3 of strike: BEST to operate 180 special bus trips for NEET re-exam students
content: The strike, called by the BEST Sanyukt Kamgar Kruti Samiti, has virtually paralysed the civic-run bus transport system since June 19, forcing passengers to depend on other transportation methods for their daily commute

title: Yoga Offers Path To Wellness Amid Demanding Work Schedules: Chief Justice
content: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant on Sunday said yoga offers a timeless framework to achieve harmony between the mind, body, and spirit and can play a crucial role in helping individuals cope with stress and demanding professional responsibilities.

title: Caller Demands Rs 30,000 For Alleged NEET Paper. Cops Probe Claim
content: The unidentified caller allegedly sent across a QR code for money transfer, promising to share what he claimed was the NEET paper.

title: Ray-Ban Heir Escalates Fight For Control Of Family's $11.5 Billion Fortune
content: Del Vecchio is attempting to buy the combined 25% stakes held by his siblings Luca and Paola in Delfin.

title: UK PM Keir Starmer May Quit Next Week, Top Contenders Who Could Replace Him
content: The speculation about his exit has grown after former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election, defeating Reform UK's Robert Kenyon by more than 9,000 votes.

title: A $14 Billion Market Takes Off As Retirees Aren't Moving To Hometowns Anymore
content: India's senior living market was valued at $3.55 billion in 2025. It is projected to grow from $4.47 billion in 2026 to $14.14 billion by 2031.

title: "F**k. It's Big A** Dog": Video Shows US Cops Shooting Pet After 911 Call
content: The Los Angeles Police Department had received a radio call from a woman who said that her neighbour had been "screaming 'Oh my God' for 20 minutes".

title: India-EU Trade Deal Likely By December, Rollout By February-March 2027
content: On January 27 this year, India and the EU announced the conclusion of negotiations for the 'mother of all deals'.

title: 22 Lakh Students, 5,440 Centres, 1 Lakh Cameras: NEET Re-Exam Begins
content: The re-test will be held from 2 pm to 5.15 pm. Candidates with disabilities, who are eligible for compensatory time, will be allowed time till 6.20 pm.

title: Students Sexually Abused, Forced To Dig Graves, Eat Vomit At New York Boarding School
content: The lawsuit claims that students who tried to escape were tracked down and brought back using K9 dogs.

title: NCP MP Supriya Sule's Daughter Gets Married, Rahul Gandhi, Devendra Fadnavis Attend
content: The festivities, spanning from the sangeet ceremony to the wedding, saw the attendance of prominent personalities from the worlds of business, politics and entertainment.

title: Uddhav Sena Crisis May Spiral Today. Thackerays Enter Firefighting Mode
content: The rebel MPs are expected to explain today the circumstances that led to their decision to break away from the Shiv Sena (UBT).

title: India Gets 3 New Indigenous Warships, Here's Why They Are Special
content: The three naval ships are stealth frigate Dunagiri, survey vessel (large) Sanshodhak, and anti-submarine warfare shallow water craft Agray

title: Meloni's Spat With Trump Shows Italy PM's Readiness To Risk A Bigger Fight
content: Giorgia Meloni, the person who Donald Trump once praised for taking "Europe by storm", has done three things that some US allies may have thought privately but never said publicly.

title: Ukraine Strikes Key Oil Refinery 2,000 Kilometres Inside Russia Using Drones
content: Ukraine has significantly stepped up attacks on Russian oil infrastructure in recent months, with Russia's crude-processing rates falling to its lowest level in two decades so far in June.

title: Spain PM's Wife To Face Corruption Trial, Told To Surrender Passport
content: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez's wife Begona Gomez is accused of using her position to influence government contracts given to a group of technology companies.

title: "Misanthropy": Millions In Brazil Get Fake Government Mobile Alert, Hack Suspected
content: Officials said the false messages were "remotely ordered by someone outside the national system of protection and civil defense," and delivered to "various regions of the country."

title: Light Rain Brings Relief In Mumbai After Monsoon Delay
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has indicated favourable conditions for further advancement of the southwest monsoon over parts of Maharashtra.

title: In A First For Monarchs, King Charles To Reveal Personal Tax Bill
content: British monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties on a voluntary basis for decades.

title: Beyond books: How a Kolkata school is teaching girls to build self-reliant futures
content: The residential school for girls uses practical learning and skill development to prepare students from rural communities for self-sustaining livelihoods

title: Telegram | Mass messaging and more
content: The ‘WhatsApp alternative’ with a strong commitment to user privacy, which has over 15 crore users in India, is facing a week-long ban in the country

title: Group of Seven | A platform for the globalised elite
content: The grouping of industrialised nations, founded in the 1970s, has weathered major geopolitical storms across the 20th and 21st centuries to remain a key forum for discussions and decision-making on global conflicts and economic challenges

title: Amid global uncertainties, parliamentary panel to study evolving economic conditions in India
content: The examination is expected to cover topics, including economic growth, inflation, employment, investment trends, fiscal management, banking sector developments, trade and the impact of global developments on India

title: Do the police need court permission for further investigation after filing the final report?
content: Although an application seeking further investigation had been filed before the concerned magistrate for a third time, no order granting permission was placed on record, the Court said

title: Congress slams Tharoor for showing ‘admiration’ for Modi; MP says statement given ‘partisan twist’
content: The row was sparked by Shashi Tharoor's reported remarks that in meetings with U.S. President Donald Trump, PM Modi conveyed that in wartime, civilian sailors on commercial ships should not be targets. Pawan Khera says Tharoor can now hear what Modi doesn’t even say.

title: India is now fourth largest economy, says govt.
content: The government says that with the GDP valued at $4.18 trillion, “India has surpassed Japan to become the world’s fourth largest economy”, it says that the country is “poised to displace Germany from the third rank” with a projected GDP of $7.3 trillion by 2030

title: Modi’s 12 years in office just a start: Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister lavishes praise on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, says BJP and NDA will rule the country for many years to come; highlights Centre’s role in temple developments, military modernisation and farmers’ welfare

title: Decoupling footpaths from accidents
content: Justice Narasimha’s judgment divorces the footpath from the theme of motor accidents. The sole objective of a footpath is not to avoid accidents. The pathways have an identity and a greater purpose of their own

title: Engineer’s body recovered from Varthur STP; search continues for another missing worker
content: The search is still underway for Brijesh Kumar, a worker from Bihar, who also went missing after the incident on Thursday evening.

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls himself ‘honorary cockroach’, urges government to hear voices of youth
content: Voicing support for the online cockroach movement by the self-styled Cockroach Janata Party, Sonam Wangchuk said the campaign should be viewed as a form of democratic feedback and not as a threat

title: I don’t think the ruling party has the stomach for sarcasm, says Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the ‘Cockroach Janta Party’, alleges threats and censorship following the takedown of his youth-led satirical political outfit’s website

title: Centre offering Ladakh unique governance model, demand for Statehood to continue: Leh Apex Body leader
content: Cherring Dorjay Lakruk, co-convenor of the Leh Apex Body, says, “We are not abandoning our demand for Statehood. Even if at present, Statehood is not possible, we will continue with the demand.”

title: CID questions Trinamool veteran Sobhandeb in signature forgery case
content: He was the party’s original choice as the Leader of Opposition before a majority of MLAs sided with Ritabrata Banerjee
